mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a comprehensive machine learning toolkit for Kubernetes, designed to facilitate the entire AI lifecycle. It offers modular components that can be used independently or as a complete platform, making it suitable for various AI use cases. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for Kubernetes expertise.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
The Kubeflow CLI (kfctl) is essential for managing your Kubeflow deployment. This step installs the CLI tool.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kfctl/releases/download/v1.10.0/kfctl_v1.10.0_linux.tar.gz

tar -xvf kfctl_v1.10.0_linux.tar.gz

sudo mv kfctl /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Kubeflow CLI for Linux.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configuring kubeconfig allows you to interact with your new Kubernetes cluster using kubectl.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> Fetch the credentials for the created cluster.

steps.title: Deploy Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step deploys Kubeflow to your Kubernetes cluster using the CLI.

```bash

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/refs/tags/v1.10.0.tar.gz

```

> Deploy Kubeflow using the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
After deployment, verify that all nodes are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow

```

> Check the status of all Kubeflow pods.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
Configure additional features like autoscaling and monitoring as needed.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply monitoring configurations.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

kfctl delete -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/refs/tags/v1.10.0.tar.gz

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> Delete the Kubeflow deployment.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your data and configurations. This step will upgrade your Kubeflow installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kubeflow > kubeflow-backup.yaml

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/refs/tags/v1.10.0.tar.gz

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl and ensure it is in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig context is not set correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --zone=us-central1-a' to set the context.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more pods are failing to start due to configuration errors.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeflow' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the machine type in your GKE cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new ML frameworks, improved integration with Kubernetes 1.30.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure to update your configurations to match the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see all Kubeflow components running with 'kubectl get pods -n kubeflow' showing 'Running' status for all pods.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a GCP account and the necessary permissions to create Kubernetes clusters.
